metadata.title: Entrepreneurship &Side Hustles
metadata.subtitle: Building Your Own Path to Financial Success

content.title: Entrepreneurship &Side Hustles
content.subtitle: Building Your Own Path to Financial Success

content.label: The Challenge
content.question:
Jordan, a CS major, noticed classmates struggling with programming and turned this observation into a tutoring side hustle, earning $800-1K monthly while working just 10 hours a week around his classes.

What if you could turn your skills and passions into income—while keeping your day job or building something entirely your own?

slides.notes:
Discussion prompts:
• "What problems do you see around you that people might pay to have solved?"
• Preview how side hustles can start small and grow based on real customer needs.

objectives.description: Recognize entrepreneurial opportunities aligned with your personal skills, interests, and market needs.
Comprehend the essential components of a business plan and minimum viable product (MVP) development.
Create strategies for balancing side hustles with traditional employment and other responsibilities.
Learn financial planning and resource allocation strategies for launching and growing new ventures.

terms.definition: The activity of setting up and running a business, taking on financial risks in the hope of profit.
terms.example: Starting a web design agency, opening a bakery, launching an app company

terms.definition: A way to make money alongside your primary job, often leveraging personal skills or interests.
terms.example: Freelance writing on weekends, tutoring after work, selling crafts online

terms.definition: A version of a product with just enough features to be usable by early customers and provide feedback.
terms.example: Test your idea quickly and cheaply before investing significant time or money.

terms.definition: Building a company from the ground up using personal finances and revenue—no outside investors.
terms.example: You maintain full ownership and control of your business decisions and profits.

leftPanel.title: Creating Value Through Innovation
paragraphs: Entrepreneurs identify opportunities, secure resources, and take calculated risks to create value through new products, services, or ways of doing business.
Entrepreneurship offers an alternative or complement to traditional employment—allowing you to pursue passions and create additional income streams.

highlightBox.text: � In today's economy, entrepreneurial skills are valuable even if you never start a business—they create adaptability and resilience.

paragraphs: See problems as opportunities. Identify needs others miss.
Evaluate risks vs. rewards. Make informed decisions.
Bounce back from setbacks. Learn from failures.
Pivot when needed. Adjust based on feedback.
Find creative solutions with limited resources.
Move from idea to implementation quickly.
Constantly seek new knowledge and skills.

layoutData.title: Where Do Business Ideas Come From?
paragraphs: Successful entrepreneurs find opportunities by looking at their own experiences, skills, and observations of problems around them.
bulletPoints: Personal Experience: Problems you've encountered needing better solutions
Skills & Expertise: Areas where you have specialized knowledge
Hobbies & Interests: Activities you enjoy and understand well
Market Trends: Emerging needs or changes in consumer behavior
Technology Changes: New capabilities enabling different products
Underserved Markets: Groups whose needs aren't fully met

keyPoint.text: 💡 Key Question: What problem do I see every day that I'm uniquely positioned to solve?

leftPanel.title: Not All Ideas Make Good Businesses
paragraphs: Before investing time and money, evaluate your idea using these criteria:
Problem Validation: Is there a real problem? How painful is it?
Market Size: How many people have this problem?
Feasibility: Do you have the skills and resources needed?
Profitability: Can you deliver at a price that makes profit?

infoCard.title: Also Consider:
items: Scalability: Can it grow beyond you?

layoutData.title: Diversifying Your Income Creates Security
paragraphs: Relying on a single income source is risky. Side hustles provide financial resilience and accelerate wealth building.
bulletPoints: Extra Income: Average side hustler earns $810/month additional
Debt Payoff: Use extra income to eliminate debt faster
Emergency Fund: Build financial cushion more quickly
Skills Development: Marketable experience for career growth
Business Asset: Potential to sell or scale into full-time

keyPoint.text: 💰 $810/month invested at 8% for 30 years = $1.1 million—side hustles can fund your retirement.

slides.notes:
Common Misconceptions:

| Misconception | Clarification |
|--------------|---------------|
| "I need a completely original idea to start a business." | Most successful businesses improve on existing solutions rather than inventing entirely new categories. Focus on solving problems better for a specific audience. |
| "I need a lot of money to start a side hustle." | Many successful side hustles start with minimal investment by leveraging existing skills and bootstrapping. Service-based businesses often require almost no startup capital. |
| "If my idea is good, customers will come automatically." | Even great products need marketing and customer discovery. Testing your idea with real potential customers before fully building it is essential. |

leftPanel.title: Take Action: Start Small and Learn
paragraphs: 1. Market Research: Interview potential customers about their needs before building anything.
2. Create MVP: Build the simplest version that delivers value and get feedback.
3. Basic Business Plan: Outline your value proposition, target market, and revenue model.
4. Start Small: Serve a few customers, document what works, then gradually expand.

scenario.name: Jordan's Tutoring Side Hustle
paragraphs: Jordan, a CS major, noticed classmates struggling with programming. Instead of just helping friends for free, he saw a business opportunity.
His approach:

scenario.name: Maya's Jewelry Business Journey
paragraphs: Maya worked full-time as an admin assistant while making jewelry as a hobby. Friends encouraged her to sell, so she started on Etsy with 10 designs.
Strategic growth over 3 years:
After saving 6 months of expenses, she went full-time.

content.question:
What made Jordan's and Maya's side hustles successful?

What strategies did they use to minimize risk while growing their businesses?

What skills, interests, or problems in your life could become a side hustle opportunity?

What would be your first step to test this idea?

questions.question: What's the difference between entrepreneurship and a side hustle? When might someone choose one over the other?
What is an MVP, and why is it important to create one before investing heavily in a business idea?
Name three characteristics of the entrepreneurial mindset. Which do you think is most important?
How can a side hustle improve your financial security beyond just the extra income it generates?

slides.notes:
Answer Key:

1. Entrepreneurship is building a full business (often primary income source), while a side hustle generates additional income alongside a job. Choose entrepreneurship when: ready for full-time commitment, have significant savings/runway, validated strong market demand. Choose side hustle when: want to test an idea first, need stable income, limited time/resources available.

2. MVP (Minimum Viable Product) lets you test assumptions and gather feedback before investing heavily. Important because: validates actual customer demand, minimizes financial risk, allows pivoting based on real feedback, prevents building features nobody wants.

3. Entrepreneurial mindset characteristics: risk-taking, resilience, adaptability, resourcefulness, action orientation, continuous learning. Responses will vary on which is most important, but look for thoughtful justification.

4. Beyond extra income, side hustles improve security by: developing new marketable skills, creating multiple income streams (diversification), building business assets that can be sold, expanding professional network, providing experience for career advancement.

takeaways.title: Start With Your Strengths
takeaways.description: The best side hustles leverage skills you already have—turning expertise into income

takeaways.title: Test Before You Invest
takeaways.description: Create an MVP, validate demand with real customers, then scale gradually

takeaways.title: Multiple Income Streams = Security
takeaways.description: Side hustles diversify your income and accelerate wealth building

takeaways.title: Entrepreneurial Skills Transfer
takeaways.description: Even if you never start a business, these skills make you more valuable in any career

main.title: � Business Idea Brainstorm
main.description: In the activity packet, you'll brainstorm potential side hustle ideas based on your skills, interests, and observations of problems around you. You'll evaluate your top ideas using the opportunity assessment criteria and create an initial action plan.

steps: List your skills, interests, and expertise
Select your top idea to develop
Create a basic action plan with first steps

content.tagline: Building Financial Futures, One Lesson at a Time
